mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, prompting risk-off behavior and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or UNRATE fails to correlate with SPX returns (Pearson r < -0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in consumer spending (CPIAUCSL) and housing starts (HOUST) signal weak aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of CPIAUCSL and HOUST on SPX returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10), or individual proxies show opposing directional impacts.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in industrial production (INDPRO) above 112.0 signals robust supply-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns during expansionary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX returns in high INDPRO regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or INDPRO fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 135,000 jobs signals a tightening labor market, increasing inflationary pressures and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or PAYEMS shows no correlation with SPX returns (Pearson r > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) and declining retail sales (RSAFS) signals stagflationary risks, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of CPIAUCSL and RSAFS on SPX returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10), or individual proxies show opposing directional impacts.

mechanism_narrative: A dovish shift in monetary policy (FEDFUNDS decline) during a recessionary regime reduces real interest rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between FEDFUNDS and GDPC1 fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05), or SPX returns in recessionary easing periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 112.0 during a post-shock recovery phase signals strong supply-side resilience, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in high INDPRO regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or INDPRO fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05).
